texas_v10_rule_basis: Preserve noindex until all critical families are verified
District-grade routing must use direct local or district-owned evidence, not generic or statewide fallback
Legacy/inventory-only evidence does not satisfy California-grade completeness

final_blockers.evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-21 targeted live probe returned HTTP 404 for the exact Kentucky Medicaid packet URL. The packet also mixed unrelated ABLE and early-intervention rows into the Medicaid family.
Reviewed 2026-06-21 Playwright render returned only "Loading to Department for Behavioral Health page." with no waiver eligibility, application, or action text.
Reviewed 2026-06-21 Playwright render returned only "Loading to Department for Behavioral Health page." with no headings, contact signals, or role-aligned DD routing content.
Kentucky still has no reviewed role-aligned Part C / First Steps source on disk after the old Early Start packet family went stale.
Reviewed 2026-06-21 targeted live probe failed DNS resolution for education.kentucky.gov, so the packet special-education root is not a live reviewed authority source.
Kentucky district routing still depends on generic statewide KDE fallback rows; no reviewed county- or district-owned special-education leaves are on disk.
The old packet used the DBHDID root as VR / Pre-ETS evidence, but that source is DD-related and does not prove vocational rehabilitation routing.
KY-SPIN is reviewed and statewide, but the fetched homepage proves parent-support and resource-linking scope rather than explicit PTI designation.
No reviewed statewide Kentucky legal-aid source exists in the current packet artifacts.
County-local packet rows still rely on stale fake-domain locator evidence or DOI mirror rows instead of reviewed county-grade official office leaves.
